title: DomComparison
description: Output of Step 8b: the polyglot-test-fixer (audit-only mode) reads the page snapshots persisted by Step 8a's playwright-tester plus the codegen-produced test files, and emits a per-TBD-constant verdict. The pipeline uses these verdicts to reclassify locator-resolution skips (so honest 'no DOM element' skips stop counting against the apply-rate gate).

file.description: Snapshot file name relative to the Step 8 workdir (page-snapshot-NN.{html,json})

tbd_constant.description: Name of the constant in the codegen locator file (e.g. GEMINI_BUTTON)

file.description: SUT-relative path of the file the constant lives in

inferred_intent.description: One-sentence semantic intent the auditor inferred from the test code (function name + assertion body)

verdict.description: matched: a real DOM element corresponds to this constant. ghost: no element exists in any snapshot. duplicate: the constant refers to the same DOM element as another constant (duplicate_of names it). low_confidence: an element exists but the auditor isn't sure it's the intended one. unevaluated: insufficient snapshot coverage to decide.

matched_selector.description: Selector the auditor would propose for a matched verdict; null otherwise

snapshot.description: Which snapshot file the match was found in

duplicate_of.description: Other tbd_constant this one is a duplicate of (when verdict=duplicate)

explanation.description: Human-readable rationale; required when verdict is ghost/duplicate/low_confidence.

extra_dom_elements.description: Optional: DOM elements the auditor noticed that look semantically related to the test plan but no codegen TBD references them. Surfaces Step 7 codegen coverage gaps.

should_exist_total.description: Count of constants that, per the auditor, refer to a DOM element that genuinely exists or should exist (i.e., excludes ghost + duplicate). This is the denominator the Step 8 apply-rate gate uses, replacing the old applied+skipped count.
